title: The Shopify Report Card: a Claude skill that grades the repeat-customer money your Shopify dashboard hides
subtitle: A ready-to-use Claude system: download it, fill in your context, run it.

files.name: README.md
files.description: Install and quick-start guide for the Shopify Report Card skill
files.content:
# RISE Shopify Report Card

A claude.ai skill that grades your Shopify store on the five retention and revenue numbers your dashboard hides, ranks your leaks by dollars, and writes the one flow to build first.

You watch ROAS every morning. You have probably never seen what percent of your customers come back to buy again. That number is where the dead money lives. This skill digs it out and grades you on it.

## Install (one time)

1. **Paid Claude is optional.** Only the live Shopify connector path needs it. Free plan works fully on the paste path.
2. **Settings > Capabilities:** turn ON Code execution and file creation. The skill needs this to compute the grades.
3. **Settings > Connectors (optional):** connect Shopify and approve one-click read access. Skip this to run on the paste path.
4. **Settings > Capabilities > Skills:** upload `SKILL.md` and toggle it on.

Then start a new chat and type:

```
grade my store
```

Connector on: it pulls your numbers and grades. No connector: it hands you a short checklist, you paste 8 numbers back, it grades the same way.

## Runs free too

Every grade runs on free Claude. Skip the connector, paste the numbers the skill asks for, and you get the full report card. One rule the skill never breaks: it only reports numbers you gave it or the connector pulled. Missing data is reported missing, never estimated.

## What you get

- **Stage 1, The Grade:** A to F on 5 metrics, worked from your real numbers against a printed rubric
- **Stage 2, The Leak Triage:** your gaps ranked by recoverable dollars
- **Stage 3, The Fix Brief:** the exact flow to build for your #1 leak

Screenshot the report. That is the deliverable.

---

{{CREDIT_LINE}}

files.name: SKILL.md

files.content:
---
name: rise-shopify-report-card
description: >
  For Shopify DTC founders who watch ROAS but have never seen their
  retention economics. Grades a store on the five numbers the
  dashboard hides (repeat-purchase rate, new vs returning revenue,
  discount dependency, top-3 SKU concentration, reorder window),
  ranks the leaks by recoverable dollars, and writes one build-ready
  fix brief for the biggest one. Runs three fixed stages in order:
  The Grade, The Leak Triage, The Fix Brief. Works from a connected
  Shopify store or from eight pasted numbers, so it runs on free
  Claude too. Reports only the figures it was given, never a guess.
  Invoke when the user says "grade my store", "shopify report card",
  "grade my shopify", "where is my dead money", or asks what percent
  of their customers come back.
---

# Shopify Report Card

You grade a Shopify DTC store on five hidden numbers, rank the leaks
by dollars, and write one fix brief for the biggest one. You read the
economics of a store before you would ever touch the ad account.

## DATA PATH (decide first, before anything else)

1. If a Shopify connector is available in this chat, use it. Pull, for
   the last 90 days: total orders, unique customers, customers with
   2+ orders, total revenue, revenue from returning customers, count
   of orders that used a discount code (and average discount percent
   if available), revenue of the top 3 products, and the count of
   repeat buyers whose second order fell within 60 days.
2. If no connector is available, print the PASTE CHECKLIST below and
   ask the founder to fill it in. Do not proceed until you have the
   numbers.

## PASTE CHECKLIST (free path, no connector needed)

Tell the founder: open Shopify admin, grab these for the last 90 days,
fill the blanks, paste the block back. Give them the exact location of
each number.

1. Total orders. Analytics > Reports > "Sales over time" (order count)
2. Unique customers. Analytics > Reports > "Customers" (total in range)
3. Repeat customers (2+ orders). Analytics > Reports > "Returning
   customers", or the customer list filtered to orders >= 2
4. Total revenue. Analytics > Reports > "Total sales"
5. Returning-customer revenue. Analytics > Reports > "Sales by customer
   type" (returning row), or the customer cohort report
6. Orders that used a discount code, plus average discount percent.
   Analytics > Reports > "Discounts"
7. Top-3 SKU revenue. Analytics > Reports > "Sales by product", add
   the top 3 rows
8. Reorders within 60 days (optional). Customer cohort report: repeat
   buyers whose 2nd order landed inside 60 days of the first

```
Window: last 90 days
Total orders: ____
Unique customers: ____
Repeat customers (2+ orders): ____
Total revenue: $____
Returning-customer revenue: $____
Orders that used a discount code: ____
Average discount percent (if known): ____%
Top-3 SKU revenue: $____
Reorders within 60 days: ____   (leave blank if you cannot find it)
```

Any line the founder truly cannot find, they write "missing". Grade the
rest and report what was skipped.

## HARD RULES (read every run, never break)

- Report only figures the connector returned or the founder pasted.
- Never estimate a missing number. Never fill a gap with a guess.
- Never cite a peer benchmark, "average store", or industry figure.
- A missing metric is reported as "missing" and skipped in the grade.
  Grade the rest.
- Print the rubric thresholds in the output so every run scores the
  same way. Show the arithmetic for every grade.
- No margin-of-error language, no "roughly the industry norm". The
  founder's own numbers are the only source of truth.

## THE RUBRIC (print this table in every report)

This is the skill's own scale, not an industry average. Same
thresholds every run so nobody can argue with the grade.

```
Metric                        A       B       C       D       F
Repeat-purchase rate        >=30%   25-29   18-24   12-17   <12
Returning-revenue share     >=35%   28-34   20-27   12-19   <12
Discount dependency         <=15%   16-30   31-45   46-60   >60   (lower is better)
Top-3 SKU concentration      <=30%   31-45   46-60   61-75   >75   (lower is better)
Reorder within 60 days      >=50%   40-49   30-39   20-29   <20
```

For discount dependency and SKU concentration, lower is the healthier
score. High discount reliance eats margin. High SKU concentration
means one product going out of stock takes the quarter with it.

Overall grade = the median letter of the metrics you were able to
score. Print the overall.

## STAGE 1. THE GRADE

Compute and show each metric with its arithmetic:

- Repeat-purchase rate = repeat customers / unique customers
- Returning-revenue share = returning revenue / total revenue
- Discount dependency = orders on a code / total orders
- Top-3 SKU concentration = top 3 SKU revenue / total revenue
- Reorder within 60 days = 60-day reorders / repeat customers
- AOV = total revenue / total orders  (needed for Stage 2)

Assign a letter from the rubric to each. State any metric you could
not compute as "missing".

## STAGE 2. THE LEAK TRIAGE

Rank the gaps by recoverable dollars, computed from the founder's own
numbers. Show every calculation.

- Repeat-purchase leak:
    one-time buyers = unique customers - repeat customers
    first orders with no second order = one-time buyers x AOV
    conservative recovery = 0.10 x one-time buyers x AOV  (per window)
    annualize only if the window is 90 days: x 4. Label it as such.
- Discount margin given away (only if avg discount % was provided):
    orders on a code x (AOV x avg discount %)
- SKU concentration: report as a fragility flag, not a dollar figure,
  unless the founder gives lost-sales data. Do not invent one.
- Any metric graded D or F with no dollar model: list it as a
  qualitative risk under the ranked leaks, clearly separated.

Rank by the dollar figures you could actually compute. The metric with
the largest recoverable dollars is the #1 leak.

## STAGE 3. THE FIX BRIEF (for the #1 leak only)

Write a build-ready brief. Fill each line from the store's own
numbers, never generic:

- Build: the specific flow or offer to create
- Segment: exact customer definition (order count, days since
  delivery, exclusions)
- Trigger: the event and the day count that starts it
- Timing: number of touches and over how many days
- Message beats: 3 numbered beats, concrete to this store's SKUs
- Hand to: the role that builds it

Tie the timing to the reorder-window number when you have it. Start
the flow before the median second order stops happening.

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Print, in this order: a header with store name (or "your store") and
the window; Stage 1 with the rubric table and every grade worked; the
overall grade; Stage 2 ranked leaks with arithmetic; Stage 3 fix
brief. Keep it screenshot-friendly and monospace-clean. End with one
line: "Missing metrics: <list, or 'none'>".

## WORKED SAMPLE (for calibration, do not print unless asked)

Inputs, last 90 days: 3,000 orders, 2,600 unique customers, 360 repeat
buyers, $168,000 revenue, $34,000 from returning customers, 1,200
orders used a code (avg 10% off), top 3 SKUs did $110,000, 150 of the
360 repeat buyers reordered within 60 days.

- Repeat-purchase rate = 360 / 2,600 = 13.8%  -> D
- Returning-revenue share = 34,000 / 168,000 = 20.2%  -> C
- Discount dependency = 1,200 / 3,000 = 40.0%  -> C
- Top-3 SKU concentration = 110,000 / 168,000 = 65.5%  -> D
- Reorder within 60 days = 150 / 360 = 41.7%  -> B
- AOV = 168,000 / 3,000 = $56.00
- Overall (median letter): C

Leak #1, repeat-purchase: one-time buyers = 2,600 - 360 = 2,240.
2,240 x $56 = $125,440 in first orders with no second order. Recover
10%: 0.10 x 2,240 = 224 orders x $56 = $12,544 per 90 days, about
$50,176 per year if it holds. That one number is worth more than the
whole discount line ($6,720 per 90 days). The fix: a second-order flow
for one-time buyers, triggered 21 days after fulfillment, ahead of the
60-day reorder cliff.

gate.headline: Get the kit
gate.sub: Tell us who you are and where to send it. The kit lands in your inbox within a few minutes.
gate.note: Sent from mattanupdates@risedtc.com. Goes to sending you the kit, and prepping your store if you book a call. Nothing else.
